A welfare check in Kititas County, Washington turns heated when a man douses a detective in lighter fluid and nearly sets him ablaze. But the tables turn when a spark ignites the suspect before he makes a daring escape.

Deputies later discovered a water bottle in Richie's van filled with what smelled like gasoline, stuffed with paper towels, with one positioned upright like a fuse. In a press release from the Kititas County Sheriff's Office Facebook page, they shared that 56-year-old Richie Allen Davis and Detective Hoffman emerged from the incident uninjured. It also states that Richie was booked on his warrants and on recommended charges of felony eluding and attempted murder. According to multiple news reports, he's currently being held on a $1 million bail, and the case is ongoing.
